old_claim.note: Standardized Road Milestones provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because surveyed road maps and itineraries can record routes and distances without requiring later standardized Roman milestone infrastructure.

source_shape.source_locator: Road maps section describing itineraries and the Peutinger Table as route records with towns, lodging, and distances.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes Roman road itineraries and map records as documents cataloging routes, towns, lodgings, and distances, not as a technology dependent on milestone installation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old edge made one road-administration artifact a prerequisite for another. The source supports route documentation as its own record practice, so milestones are not required for the scoped map concept.

ontology_before: Surveyed road maps inherited a later milestone dependency, making a broad cartographic record depend on a narrower road-marker deployment.
ontology_after: Surveyed road maps keep itinerary and geometry prerequisites while standardized milestones remain a separate road-network administration technology.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: The graph now permits road mapping and itinerary records without forcing a later Roman milestone dependency.
would_reject_if: The dependent node were narrowed to maps compiled specifically from milestone inscriptions.
A source showed the first scoped road maps required standardized milestone posts as measurement inputs.
A future split introduced a separate milestone-derived Roman road-map node.
short_reason: Road maps and itineraries do not require standardized milestone infrastructure.
